about.title: Tea aroma, mountain character
about.body: Aroadri comes from Aroma and Adri, a Sanskrit word meaning mountain. The identity meets the visual language of shanshui: mountain, cloud, and water as symbols of calm, purity, and craft.

pillars.title: Nature Aroma
pillars.desc: Each drink is designed to carry a clean, gentle tea aroma.

pillars.title: Adri
pillars.desc: Adri means mountain, the inspiration behind the logo, store atmosphere, and calm character.

pillars.title: Modern Chinese Tea
pillars.desc: Oolong, jasmine green, ceylon black, roasted yellow, and biluochun are served in a modern format.

locations.title: Aroadri Tea Locations
locations.subtitle: Two active outlets in the Malioboro area, Yogyakarta.
locations.empty: No active outlet is available yet.

terms.title: Terms and Conditions of Use
terms.intro: These Terms and Conditions of Use (the “Terms”) are a valid and legally binding agreement between you (the “Member” or “User”) and PT. Gandha Hill Catering Management Indonesia (the “Company”), the rights holder of the Aroadri Tea brand. By registering an account, accessing, or using any provided service, the User is deemed to have read, understood, agreed to, and be bound by these Terms as contemplated under Article 1320 of the Indonesian Civil Code regarding the validity of agreements.

sections.title: Definitions and Parties
sections.body: “Company” means PT. Gandha Hill Catering Management Indonesia, a limited liability company established under the laws of the Republic of Indonesia and domiciled in the Special Region of Yogyakarta, as owner and operator of the Aroadri Tea brand and all related sales channels, systems, and services. “Service” includes the official website, membership program, digital card, points, vouchers, promotions, outlet ordering, after-sales support, and all supporting technology systems. “Member” means an individual registered in the Company system. The User expressly represents that the User has legal capacity and authority to be bound by these Terms.

sections.title: Registration, Eligibility, and Member Representations
sections.body: The Member represents and warrants that all submitted data is true, accurate, current, and complete, and must update it when changes occur. The Company may, at its sole discretion, reject, suspend, limit, or deactivate registration without prior notice if it finds false, duplicate, or unverifiable data; indications of misuse, fraud, or bad faith; breach of these Terms; or any matter considered harmful to the Company, other Members, or third parties.

sections.title: Use of Services at Outlets
sections.body: To receive membership benefits during outlet transactions, the Member must provide the registered phone number and confirm the matching name shown in the system. Points, vouchers, and other benefits may only be recorded for valid transactions validated in the Company point-of-sale system at the time of transaction. Retroactive claims for completed transactions or transactions whose books have been closed are not accommodated unless the Company identifies a recording error by the Company.

sections.title: Points, Vouchers, and Loyalty Programs
sections.body: Point earning, validity, redemption rules, minimum transaction thresholds, and exclusions for points or vouchers follow the active program published by the Company. All benefits are non-cash, personal, and conditional. They cannot be exchanged for money, transferred without written Company approval, and may be cancelled if the underlying transaction is cancelled, refunded, or indicated as fraudulent. The Company may change, suspend, or end loyalty programs with reasonable notice.

sections.title: Account Security and Member Obligations
sections.body: Members are fully responsible for maintaining confidentiality of account credentials, including passwords and one-time passwords (OTP). Members must not use accounts for unlawful purposes, register another person’s identity without valid authority, manipulate transactions, scrape, reverse engineer, decompile, attempt unauthorized access, distribute malware, disrupt service integrity, or violate Law No. 11 of 2008 on Electronic Information and Transactions as amended. Activity through a Member account is the Member’s responsibility unless the Member proves otherwise to the Company.

sections.title: Verification, Correction, and Audit
sections.body: The Company may verify and audit receipts, transaction history, system logs, and related data to check loyalty-program integrity and compliance with these Terms. If recording errors, misuse, or breach are found, the Company may correct data or adjust points/vouchers, suspend or deactivate accounts, withdraw granted benefits, and/or take necessary legal action.

sections.title: Limitation of Liability
sections.body: The Company seeks to provide the Service with commercially reasonable care but does not guarantee that the Service will always be uninterrupted, error-free, or delay-free. To the extent permitted by applicable law, the Company is not liable for indirect, consequential, incidental losses, loss of profit, loss of data, or loss of business opportunity arising from the use of or inability to use the Service. Company liability is limited to restoration of valid benefits and/or data correction under internal Company procedures.

sections.title: Changes to These Terms
sections.body: The Company may add, reduce, or amend these Terms at any time for operational needs, regulatory changes, or security considerations. Changes apply from the effective date stated and published on the official Company website. Continued use of the Service after changes take effect is deemed Member acceptance of the latest version.

sections.title: Intellectual Property Rights
sections.body: All intellectual property rights in the brand, logo, trade name, designs, promotional materials, software, recipes, and other Aroadri Tea content are protected by Law No. 28 of 2014 on Copyright, Law No. 20 of 2016 on Marks and Geographical Indications, and related regulations. Members may not copy, reproduce, distribute, publicly display, modify, or create derivative works without written Company permission. Any violation may be processed civilly or criminally under applicable law.

sections.title: Underage Members
sections.body: This Service is intended for users who are at least 17 years old or otherwise legally adult. Registration by children as defined in Article 1 point 5 of Law No. 27 of 2022 on Personal Data Protection requires consent from a lawful parent or guardian. If registration without such consent is found, the Company may deactivate the account and delete the data without prior notice.

sections.title: Force Majeure
sections.body: The Company is not responsible for delay or failure to perform the Service caused by events beyond the Company’s reasonable control, including natural disasters, fire, riots, sabotage, cyberattacks, public network disruption, power outages, pandemic, government policy, or other force majeure events recognized under Indonesian jurisprudence.

sections.title: Dispute Resolution
sections.body: Any dispute arising from or relating to these Terms will first be resolved by deliberation to reach consensus within 30 calendar days after written notice is delivered by either party. If amicable resolution is not reached, the parties agree to resolve the dispute through the Yogyakarta District Court as the authorized and exclusive forum.

sections.title: Governing Law and Contact
sections.body: These Terms are governed by and interpreted under the laws of the Republic of Indonesia, including the Indonesian Civil Code, Law No. 11 of 2008 on Electronic Information and Transactions as amended by Law No. 1 of 2024, Law No. 8 of 1999 on Consumer Protection, and Law No. 27 of 2022 on Personal Data Protection. Official notices, correspondence, or requests related to these Terms may be submitted through the official channels listed on the Aroadri Tea website or social channels.

privacy.title: Personal Data Protection Policy
privacy.intro: This Personal Data Protection Policy (the “Policy”) is the commitment of PT. Gandha Hill Catering Management Indonesia (the “Personal Data Controller” or the “Company”) to fulfill legal obligations under Law No. 27 of 2022 on Personal Data Protection (the “PDP Law”) and its implementing regulations, Law No. 11 of 2008 on Electronic Information and Transactions as amended, and Government Regulation No. 71 of 2019 on Electronic System and Transaction Operation. This Policy is an integral part of the Aroadri Tea Terms and Conditions of Use.

sections.title: Identity of the Personal Data Controller
sections.body: The Personal Data Controller under this Policy is PT. Gandha Hill Catering Management Indonesia, a limited liability company established under and subject to the laws of the Republic of Indonesia, domiciled in the Special Region of Yogyakarta, and operator of the Aroadri Tea brand. The Personal Data Controller is responsible for determining the purposes and control of Personal Data processing as referred to in Article 1 point 4 of the PDP Law.

sections.title: Definition of Personal Data and Data Subject
sections.body: “Personal Data” means data about an identified or identifiable individual, alone or combined with other information, directly or indirectly, through electronic or non-electronic systems, as referred to in Article 1 point 1 of the PDP Law. “Personal Data Subject” or “Member” means the individual to whom the Personal Data relates.

sections.title: Types of Personal Data Processed
sections.body: General Personal Data processed may include full name, email address, phone number, city of domicile, birth date if provided, language preference, transaction history, membership status, points, vouchers, IP address, device logs, and customer-service communication records. Specific Personal Data as referred to in Article 4 paragraph (2) of the PDP Law is not actively collected from Members through the Service, and if inadvertently provided will be protected at the highest level required by the PDP Law.

sections.title: Legal Basis and Purposes of Processing
sections.body: Personal Data processing is carried out based on one or more lawful bases under Article 20 paragraph (2) of the PDP Law, including explicit valid consent from the Personal Data Subject, performance of the Service agreement, fulfillment of Controller legal obligations, public-interest tasks only where relevant, and legitimate Controller interests while respecting the rights and freedoms of the Personal Data Subject. Processing purposes include account verification, transaction recording, loyalty-program operation, dispute resolution, financial audit, fraud prevention, tax reporting compliance, and relevant communication.

sections.title: Rights of Personal Data Subjects
sections.body: Under Articles 5 through 14 of the PDP Law, Members have the right to obtain clear information about Controller identity, legal basis, and processing purposes; complete, update, and/or correct their Personal Data; access and obtain copies of their Personal Data; end processing, delete, and/or destroy their Personal Data; withdraw processing consent; object to processing that results in decisions based on profiling and/or automated processing; delay or restrict processing proportionally; claim compensation for processing violations; and obtain, use, and transmit their Personal Data in a commonly used format.

sections.title: Data Sharing and Personal Data Processors
sections.body: The Controller may appoint Personal Data Processors supporting Service operations, including cloud hosting, managed databases, transactional email, content delivery networks, cybersecurity services, and off-site backups. Each Processor is contractually bound to process data according to Controller instructions and maintain Personal Data confidentiality as referred to in Article 51 of the PDP Law. The Company does not sell Member Personal Data. Disclosure to third parties outside Processors occurs only when required by law or upon official request from competent authorities.

sections.title: Personal Data Security
sections.body: The Controller applies technical and organizational measures under the accountability principle in Article 16 of the PDP Law, including role-based access control, multi-factor authentication for internal accounts handling Personal Data, encryption and/or hashing of sensitive Personal Data, immutable audit logs, documented periodic backups with retention, and incident and vulnerability monitoring. The Personal Data Subject understands that no electronic security method is completely risk-free, and the Controller does not guarantee security in circumstances outside its reasonable control.

sections.title: Storage, Retention, and Deletion
sections.body: Personal Data is stored while the Member account is active; while needed for Service performance and/or legal obligations, including bookkeeping obligations under Article 11 of the Indonesian Commercial Code; while needed to defend Controller rights and legal interests; or as determined by competent authorities. After the retention period ends, the Controller will delete, destroy, or anonymize Personal Data according to Article 43 of the PDP Law.

sections.title: Personal Data Breach Notification
sections.body: If a Personal Data protection failure occurs as referred to in Article 46 of the PDP Law, the Controller will notify the Personal Data Subject and the Personal Data Protection Agency in writing no later than 3 calendar days after the failure is known, explain the affected Personal Data, the time and manner of the incident, and the handling and recovery steps taken, and perform timely mitigation and recovery.

sections.title: Cross-Border Personal Data Transfer
sections.body: If Personal Data processing requires transfer to a jurisdiction outside Indonesia, the Controller ensures that the receiving country has a protection level equal to or higher than the PDP Law or applies binding contractual clauses under Article 56 of the PDP Law. If those requirements are not met, transfer is carried out only with explicit consent from the Personal Data Subject after sufficient information is provided.

sections.title: Cookies and Similar Tracking Technologies
sections.body: The Company website uses cookies and/or local storage to maintain authentication sessions, store language preferences, fulfill security measures, and keep Service features running smoothly. Strictly necessary cookies cannot be disabled without risking Service malfunction. Members may manage or delete cookies through their browser settings.

sections.title: Children’s Data
sections.body: Processing of children’s Personal Data observes Article 25 of the PDP Law, which requires special consent from a lawful parent or guardian. The Service is not intended for children under 17 years old. If registration without such consent is detected, the Controller will delete the relevant data no later than 14 calendar days after discovery.

sections.title: Exercise of Rights and Complaints
sections.body: Requests to exercise rights referred to in Article 5 of this Policy, including access, correction, restriction, consent withdrawal, and deletion of Personal Data, may be submitted through Aroadri Tea official channels. The Controller will respond no later than 3 x 24 hours as referred to in Article 13 of the PDP Law, with reasonable extension where needed for identity verification. If the Personal Data Subject considers processing non-compliant, the subject may file a complaint with the Personal Data Protection Agency as regulated in Articles 58 through 65 of the PDP Law.

sections.title: Policy Changes and Governing Law
sections.body: The Controller may update this Policy from time to time according to operational, technological, or regulatory changes. The latest version will be published on the Company website with the effective date stated. This Policy is governed by and interpreted under the laws of the Republic of Indonesia.

careers.subtitle: We are always looking for new friends for our Yogyakarta outlets and back office team. See open positions below and apply directly from this page.
